annotations.label: Orientability (informal)

anchor.snippet: orientability is a property of some topological spaces

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of orientability for general topological spaces; only linear-algebraic `Orientation` of a module exists.

annotations.label: Orientation of a space

anchor.snippet: a choice between them is an orientation of the space

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Orientation R M ι` formalizes the vector-space analogue (a ray in the alternating-form space), not orientation of a general topological space.

annotations.label: Non-orientable space

anchor.snippet: A space is non-orientable if

annotations.note: No topological notion of non-orientability exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Möbius strip is non-orientable

anchor.snippet: A Möbius strip is an example of a non-orientable space

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the Möbius strip as a topological object.

annotations.label: Orientable surface in Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: is orientable if a chiral two-dimensional figure

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of orientable embedded surface in Euclidean space.

annotations.label: Orientable abstract surface

anchor.snippet: An abstract surface (i.e., a two-dimensional manifold ) is orientable if a consistent concept of clockwise rotation can be defined

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define orientability for abstract 2-manifolds.

annotations.label: Equivalence with no Möbius subset

anchor.snippet: This turns out to be equivalent to the question of whether the surface contains no subset that is homeomorphic to the Möbius strip

annotations.note: Not present; requires both the Möbius strip and surface orientability, neither of which are formalized.

annotations.label: Orientation and oriented surface

anchor.snippet: a consistent choice of "clockwise" (as opposed to counter-clockwise) is called an orientation

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Module.Oriented` records a chosen orientation on a module, the linear-algebra analogue of the surface notion.

annotations.label: Two orientations on an orientable surface

anchor.snippet: an orientable surface admits exactly two orientations

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the vector-space analogue (two orientations on a finite-dim space), not the surface statement.

annotations.label: Orientable and non-orientable surface examples

anchor.snippet: Spheres , planes , and tori are orientable

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have orientability of sphere/plane/torus as manifolds.

annotations.label: Embedding obstructions

anchor.snippet: The real projective plane and Klein bottle cannot be embedded

annotations.note: The Klein bottle and ℝP² and their (non-)embeddability are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two-sided vs orientable

anchor.snippet: the property of being orientable is not equivalent to being two-sided

annotations.note: Two-sidedness of an embedded submanifold is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triangulation of a surface

anchor.snippet: Any surface has a triangulation : a decomposition into triangles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no triangulation theory for topological surfaces.

annotations.label: Triangulation determines orientation

anchor.snippet: when glued together, neighboring edges are pointing in the opposite direction, then this determines an orientation

annotations.note: Not formalized — no triangulations and no surface orientations in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalization to n-manifolds via triangulation

anchor.snippet: This approach generalizes to any

annotations.note: Not formalized — Mathlib lacks both PL/triangulated manifolds and manifold orientability.

annotations.label: Orientability via first homology

anchor.snippet: is orientable if and only if

annotations.note: Singular homology of surfaces is present, but no manifold orientability hence no such characterization.

annotations.label: Orientability of a manifold (overview)

anchor.snippet: There are several possible definitions of what it means for M to be orientable

annotations.note: Mathlib's manifold library does not define orientability.

annotations.label: Equivalence of definitions

anchor.snippet: then M is orientable under one definition if and only if it is orientable under the others

annotations.note: Not formalized — no manifold orientability definitions exist to compare.

annotations.label: Orientation preserving transition function

anchor.snippet: When the Jacobian determinant is positive, the transition function is said to be orientation preserving

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define orientation-preserving transition maps between charts.

annotations.label: Oriented atlas and orientability (differentiable)

anchor.snippet: An oriented atlas on

annotations.note: No oriented-atlas structure exists in Mathlib's manifold framework.

annotations.label: Orientation as maximal oriented atlas

anchor.snippet: an orientation of

annotations.note: Not present — Mathlib lacks the maximal-atlas formulation of manifold orientation.

annotations.label: Orientability via tangent bundle structure group

anchor.snippet: is orientable if and only if the structure group of the tangent bundle can be reduced in this way

annotations.note: Mathlib's vector bundles do not carry structure-group reduction machinery for orientability.

annotations.label: Volume form

anchor.snippet: A volume form is a nowhere vanishing section

annotations.note: Mathlib defines a volume form on an oriented real inner product space, not as a section on a manifold.

annotations.label: Volume form equivalent to orientability

anchor.snippet: The existence of a volume form is therefore equivalent to orientability of the manifold

annotations.note: Manifold orientability is not defined, so the equivalence is not stated.

annotations.label: Right-handed basis

anchor.snippet: then the basis is said to be right-handed

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the orientation determined by a basis (i.e. whether it represents the positive orientation), the abstract version of right-handedness.

annotations.label: Orientation preserving sends right-handed to right-handed

anchor.snippet: A transition function is orientation preserving if and only if it sends right-handed bases to right-handed bases

annotations.note: Mathlib characterizes linear equivs preserving a basis-induced orientation by positivity of det, the linear-algebra version of this statement.

annotations.label: Local orientation

anchor.section: Homology and the orientability of general manifolds
anchor.snippet: A local orientation of

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks local-homology generators serving as local orientations.

annotations.label: Excision identification of local homology

anchor.section: Homology and the orientability of general manifolds
anchor.snippet: By the excision theorem

annotations.note: Excision is not yet stated for singular homology in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local homology is Z

anchor.section: Homology and the orientability of general manifolds
anchor.snippet: A computation with the long exact sequence in relative homology shows that the above homology group is isomorphic

annotations.note: Local-homology computations for manifolds are not formalized.

annotations.label: Orientation preserving on topological manifold

anchor.section: Homology and the orientability of general manifolds
anchor.snippet: On a topological manifold, a transition function is orientation preserving if

annotations.note: Topological orientation-preservation via local homology is not formalized.

annotations.label: Oriented atlas / orientability (topological)

anchor.section: Homology and the orientability of general manifolds
anchor.snippet: An oriented atlas is one for which all transition functions are orientation preserving

annotations.note: No oriented-atlas notion for topological manifolds exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orientation determines local orientations

anchor.section: Homology and the orientability of general manifolds
anchor.snippet: an orientation of

annotations.note: Not formalized — relies on local-orientation framework absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homological characterization of orientability

anchor.section: Homology and the orientability of general manifolds
anchor.snippet: Assuming that

annotations.note: Not formalized — top-dimensional homology of manifolds is not yet developed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stiefel–Whitney characterization of orientability

anchor.snippet: is orientable if and only if the first Stiefel–Whitney class

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Stiefel–Whitney classes.

annotations.label: Cohomology parametrizes orientations

anchor.snippet: parametrizes the choices of orientations

annotations.note: Not formalized — manifold cohomology with ℤ/2 coefficients indexing orientations is absent.

annotations.label: Orientation double cover

anchor.snippet: The manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib has no orientation double cover construction.

annotations.label: Structure of the orientation double cover

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] is non-orientable, however, then

annotations.note: No orientation double cover exists in Mathlib to describe.

annotations.label: Orientation of a manifold with boundary

anchor.snippet: an orientation of [MATH] is defined to be an orientation of its interior

annotations.note: Manifolds-with-boundary orientation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Induced orientation on boundary

anchor.snippet: Such an orientation induces an orientation of

annotations.note: No induced boundary orientation is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boundary orientation (smooth case)

anchor.snippet: The orientation of [MATH] is defined by the condition that a basis

annotations.note: The outward-normal-first boundary orientation rule is not formalized.

annotations.label: Orientable double cover

anchor.snippet: This covering space is called the orientable double cover

annotations.note: Mathlib has no orientable double cover construction.

annotations.label: Connectedness criterion for double cover

anchor.snippet: is connected if and only if

annotations.note: Not formalized — the orientable double cover is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Construction via orientation-preserving loops

anchor.snippet: Another way to construct this cover is to divide the loops based at a basepoint

annotations.note: Not formalized — no orientation-preserving loop subgroup of π₁ in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orientable vector bundle

anchor.snippet: is called orientable when the structure group may be reduced

annotations.note: Orientability is not yet defined for Mathlib's vector bundles.

annotations.label: Orientability of smooth manifold via tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: a smooth manifold is defined to be orientable if its tangent bundle is orientable

annotations.note: Neither tangent-bundle orientability nor smooth-manifold orientability is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle is always orientable

anchor.snippet: the tangent bundle is always orientable, even over nonorientable manifolds

annotations.note: Not formalized — depends on vector-bundle orientability, which is absent.

annotations.label: Space and time orientability

anchor.snippet: there are two kinds of orientability: space orientability and time orientability

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Lorentzian geometry or notions of space/time orientability.

annotations.label: Time-orientability characterization

anchor.snippet: a spacetime is time-orientable if and only if any two observers can agree

annotations.note: No spacetime/observer formalism exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space and time orientation characters

anchor.snippet: the pseudo-orthogonal group [MATH] has a pair of characters

annotations.note: Mathlib has no pseudo-orthogonal group O(p,q) with its characters.

annotations.label: Space orientation as section

anchor.snippet: A space-orientation of a pseudo-Riemannian manifold is identified with a section

annotations.note: Pseudo-Riemannian manifolds and their orientation bundles are not in Mathlib.
